page: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. This configuration delivers 48 physical cores operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 10.67 GB per core. It does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is limited, with stress-ng single-core benchmarks placing in the bottom 10%. Conversely, multi-core and parallel processing performance is robust. The server achieves strong results in the top 25% for Passmark CPU tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations, which reach 58.62 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth and Geekbench multi-core scores remain in the average range.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers a qualitative advantage for memory-bound applications due to its 512 GB RAM capacity. The primary tradeoff lies in the shared CPU allocation and lower single-core clock speed, which makes it less efficient for workloads requiring dedicated, high-frequency single-thread execution. However, for highly parallelized tasks, the 48-core architecture mitigates single-core limitations.

This server is best suited for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads. Appropriate use cases include large-scale caching layers, in-memory databases, parallel data processing, and multi-threaded software compilation. It is not recommended for applications requiring high single-threaded performance or local NVMe storage.
description: The UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with 48 physical cores and a shared CPU allocation. Operating at 2.0 GHz, it features a substantial 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding 10.67 GB per core, though it lacks local storage. While single-core benchmarks like stress-ng place in the bottom 10%, its multi-core and memory-bound performance is strong, with Passmark CPU scores and Redis SET operations ranking in the top 25%. This server offers high memory density, making it a cost-efficient option for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale caching, in-memory databases, and parallel data processing tasks that do not rely on high single-threaded clock speeds or local disk storage.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 server featuring 48 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB of RAM, and a KVM hypervisor. It delivers strong multi-core and Redis performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-48xCPU-512GB is an x86_64 server with 48 AMD EPYC cores and 512 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory instance featuring forty-eight cores, designed for parallel processing and large-scale in-memory database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 AMD EPYC 7542 cores with 512 GB of RAM
Strong multi-core Passmark and Redis SET performance
KVM hypervisor with shared CPU allocation
No local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Best suited for in-memory databases and caching layers
